L1.head: isothermal compressibility-assisted dynamic squeezing index perturbation engineering (iCASE)
L1.claim: iCASE enables the construction of hierarchical modular networks reflecting dynamic squeezing index

L1.head: hierarchical modular networks
L1.claim: Hierarchical networks optimize enzyme structural integrity through dynamic squeezing index representation

L1.head: machine learning models
L1.claim: Machine learning models predict optimal conditions for enzyme performance based on network data

L1.head: synergistic effect
L1.claim: Synergistic validation between chemical engineering and computational methods confirms enhanced enzyme properties

L2.head: dynamic squeezing index
L2.claim: Dynamic squeezing index provides insights into substrate-induced conformational changes

L2.head: machine learning algorithms
L2.claim: ML algorithms correlate conformational changes with catalytic efficiency metrics

L2.head: predicted binding modes
L2.claim: Predicted binding modes validate against experimental crystallographic and kinetic data

L2.head: adaptive evolution analysis
L2.claim: Adaptive evolution analysis identifies critical residues (H-bonds, salt bridges, hydrophobic interactions)

L2.head: identified interactions
L2.claim: Key residue interactions guide rational design of improved enzyme variants

L2.head: engineered mutations
L2.claim: Engineered mutations validate against catalytic assay performance metrics

L3.head: dynamic squeezing index network
L3.claim: Network identifies critical structural features like metal ions and disulfide bonds

L3.head: structural features
L3.claim: Critical features optimize active site configuration for stability/activity

L3.head: engineered enzymes
L3.claim: Engineered enzymes show improved catalytic efficiency compared to conventional designs

L3.head: protein surface charge analysis
L3.claim: Surface charge analysis correlates with phenotypic adaptive landscape predictions

L3.head: dynamic response prediction
L3.claim: Dynamic response prediction refines comprehensive enzyme engineering framework

L3.head: integrated workflow
L3.claim: Workflow validates against experimental stability/activity measurements under varied conditions
